---
title: "Development"
---

## Repository layout

```
now-playing/   Rust sidecar (the OS-facing binary)
extension/     VSCode extension shim (TypeScript)
docs/          This Quarto site
```

## Build

```bash
# Sidecar
cd now-playing
cargo build --release

# Extension
cd ../extension
npm install
npm run compile

# Place the sidecar where the extension expects it (Linux x86_64):
mkdir -p bin/linux-x64
cp ../now-playing/target/release/now-playing bin/linux-x64/
```

## Run the Extension Development Host

Open `extension/` in VSCode and press `F5`. To point at a checked-out debug
binary instead of the bundled release, set
[`nowPlaying.sidecarPath`](configuration.qmd) in your user settings.

## Packaging a VSIX

```bash
cd extension
npx vsce package
```

The CI workflow under [`.github/workflows/`](https://github.com/riccardo-enr/vscode-now-playing/tree/main/.github/workflows)
builds and attaches a `linux-x64` VSIX to tagged releases.

## Building this site

```bash
cd docs
quarto preview     # live preview
quarto render      # build to _site/
```
